title: hieracium murorum subsp. tenuiglandulosum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. tenuiglandulosum, also known as a subspecies of Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Hieracium murorum subsp. tenuiglandulosum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
